title.value: A survey on automatic generation of medical imaging reports based on deep learning
title.evidence: A survey on automatic generation of medical imaging reports based on deep learning

authors.evidence: Ting Pang1*, Peigao Li1 and Lijie Zhao1

year.evidence: Received: 19 February 2023 Accepted: 9 May 2023 Published online: 18 May 2023

abstract.value: Recent advances in deep learning have shown great potential for the automatic generation of medical imaging reports. Deep learning techniques, inspired by image captioning, have made significant progress in the field of diagnostic report generation. This paper provides a comprehensive overview of recent research efforts in deep learning-based medical imaging report generation and proposes future directions in this field. First, we summarize and analyze the data set, architecture, application, and evaluation of deep learning-based medical imaging report generation. Specially, we survey the deep learning architectures used in diagnostic report generation, including hierarchical RNN-based frameworks, attention-based frameworks, and reinforcement learning-based frameworks. In addition, we identify potential challenges and suggest future research directions to support clinical applications and decision-making using medical imaging report generation systems.

architectures.evidence: we survey the deep learning architectures used in diagnostic report generation, including hierarchical RNN-based frameworks, attention-based frameworks, and reinforcement learning-based frameworks.
architectures.notes: These architectures represent the current state-of-the-art in medical imaging report generation.

value.name: Indiana University Chest X-Ray (IU X-Ray)
value.description: Chest X-ray images paired with their corresponding diagnostic reports.

value.name: CheXpert
value.description: Chest radiographs of 65,240 patients with lung diseases.

value.name: MIMIC-CXR
value.description: Chest radiographs in DICOM format with free-text radiology reports.

value.name: PadChest
value.description: Chest X-ray data set obtained from 67,000 patients.

data_sets.evidence: The current public data sets have greatly contributed to the development of deep learning for medical imaging report generation.
data_sets.notes: These datasets are essential for training deep learning models in medical imaging.

evaluation_metrics.evidence: BLEU, ROUGE, METEOR, and CIDER are commonly used evaluation metrics for medical image report generation.
evaluation_metrics.notes: These metrics are adapted from machine translation and text summarization.

performance.value: Significant progress in the automatic generation of medical imaging reports has been achieved.
performance.evidence: Automatic generation of medical reports using deep learning is still an emerging area with many challenges.
performance.notes: The paper highlights the advancements and ongoing challenges in the field.

challenges.evidence: Despite the significant progress made in medical imaging report generation based on deep learning, this section aims to highlight the unresolved issues...
challenges.notes: These challenges need to be addressed for further advancements.

future_work.value: Establish public databases with a variety of image modalities and develop private datasets to address the limitations of medical images and complex annotations.
future_work.evidence: To address these challenges, we need to establish public databases with a variety of image modalities...
future_work.notes: Future research should focus on enhancing dataset diversity and improving annotation processes.
